original: One of the drones anchors its clawed feet in the floor and launches something from a canister on its back! The object erupts into a wide, weighted net that
"+{var0}.getCombatName()+" just barely manage"+("PlayerCharacter"!==e.getClassName()?"s":"")+" to avoid. The weights must be magnetic, as they stick to the wall as well. Nasty surprise!

original: slams into "+{var0}.getCombatName()+", smearing itself against the wall with sticky cables and magnetic weights. Your arms are pinned to your side, and your [pc.weapon] clatters to the ground. A cable connecting you to the offending bot goes taut, and its weapons level at you. Shit!

original: raises up on its legs and focuses its targeting laser on "+{var0}.getCombatName()+". You hear a whirring sound from the robot’s gun-head, and then it beeps ominously.

original: All the robots beep in unison after that, apparently sharing some sort of linked targeting!
You’re fighting a horde of security droids, controlled directly by Commander Valden.
The man you tried to save may be nothing more than a digital echo of his consciousness, but he’s more than capable of bringing all his military experience to bear against you.
has brilliant red sensors covering you, seeming to direct its kin to fight harder. The others march to its tune, firing their lasers in a hail of red streaks amid their inexorable advance.
You just barely find cover in time!
The bolt slams into you, burning horribly. The other drones seem transfixed by you for a moment before they let loose shrill noises and start charging, weapons blasting away!
drone’s speakers. “Come on! I’m a damn computer, fucking unkillable! Keep trying, and see where this gets you!”
“There we go! I’m back in the game, good as new! How about you?” "+{var1}.toTitleCase({var0})+" bellows in Valden’s voice. “Let’s dance!”
context: <> <> combatAppend("\n\n“There we go! I’m back in the game, good as new! How about you?” "+m.m.toTitleCase(e.uniqueName)+" bellows in Valden’s voice. “Let’s dance!”")

original: Each bot is a mechanical quadruped, more lizard-like than canid, with a squat body covered in sleek armor plates. Rather than a head, they have angled blast shields that face you, parted around the barrel of a gun and a laser sight that ceaselessly tracks your movements. There’s still
